seed_problem: single-machine state-coupled scheduling with asymmetric heat/cool dynamics
mutation: composite: order a queue of temperature-window jobs under paid-instant-heating vs free-but-exponentially-slowing cooling, minimizing heating energy plus weighted lateness
title: Pottery Kiln Firing Queue
strategies: input-order minimal-idle baseline (fire as given, cool just into each window, heat only to lo)
hottest-first ride-the-cooling-curve greedy (temperature-monotone; the trap -- flat cooling tail blows deadline-tight cool jobs done last)
deadline-driven / EDD ordering that ascends in temperature so the kiln heats up monotonically with almost no wasted idle
structured-candidate + adjacent-swap local search on the exact objective, inserting a reheat spike only where it is cheaper than the deadline penalty
fire-temperature shaping (fire hotter to preserve heat for the next hot job vs fire at lo to save energy) -- unexploited headroom above the reference
